research.title: ELSA3D: Elastic Semantic Anchoring for Unified 3D Understanding and Generation
research.summary: Tianjiao Yu, Xinzhuo Li, Yifan Shen et al. — Unified 3D foundation models aspire to generate 3D assets and reason about them in language within a single backbone, but their text-3D interaction remains largely implicit. Existing methods concatenate text and 3D…

research.title: Graph Convolutional Attention: A Spectral Perspective on Graph Denoising and Diffusion
research.summary: Shervin Khalafi, Igor Krawczuk, Sergio Rozada et al. — Denoising graphs is a fundamental problem in graph learning and the core operation of graph diffusion models. Attention-based architectures like graph transformers have recently shown promise in denoising graphs.…

research.title: Rethinking Indic AI from a Lens of Cultural Heritage Preservation
research.summary: Aparna Madva, Sharath Srivatsa, Srinath Srinivasa et al. — As Artificial Intelligence (AI) makes inroads into different parts of the Indian subcontinent, there is significant interest in studying how AI impacts the linguistic and cultural foundations of this civilization.

research.title: On the feasibility of dependency parsing of non-human sequences without a gold standard. Is evaluation possible in other species?
research.summary: Ramon Ferrer-i-Cancho, Catherine Hobaiter, Thore Bergman et al. — Dependency parsing consists of finding a tree representation for a sequence. Unsupervised dependency parsing aims to develop parsing methods without a gold standard during model training.

research.title: GraphBU: MILP Instance Generation with Graph-Native Block Units
research.summary: Xiaolei Guo, Chenyu Zhou, Jianghao Lin et al. — Mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) instances used for solver development are hard to obtain when models come from private or application-specific pipelines. A generator must keep the structure that solvers and…

research.title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology
research.summary: Ghassen Marrakchi, Basarab Matei — - Objective: Multimodal deep learning models in oncology are currently limited by monolithic designs that rigidly couple data ingestion, clinical routing, and artificial intelligence (AI) inference.

research.summary: Srivatsa Kulkarni, Debarag Banerjee — Fine-scale socioeconomic information is often unavailable across rapidly ur-banizing regions of the developing world, like India, limiting the ability to delineate intra-urban variations in affluence and deprivation.…

research.summary: Sambaran Bandyopadhyay, Ananth Muppidi — Multi-hop Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs faces a critical challenge: traditional retrieve-then-read pipelines break differentiability, preventing the retriever from learning to bridge the semantic gap where…

research.title: DepthWeave-KV: Token-Adaptive Cross-Layer Residual Factorization for Long-Context KV Cache Compression
research.summary: Anna Cordoba, Adam Puente Tercero, Nerea Angulo Hijo et al. — Long-context language model inference is increasingly limited by the memory bandwidth and capacity required to store key-value caches, yet existing compression methods often apply uniform budgets across layers or tokens…

research.title: Bridging Physical Reasoning and Task Generalization via Visual Action Outcome Reasoning Alignment
research.summary: Han-Jun Ko, Jr-Jen Chen, Haobo Yuan et al. — Vision-language models (VLMs) struggle to generalize in interactive physical reasoning, particularly under unseen tasks and environments. Two key failure modes are prominent: hallucinated chain-of-thought (CoT)…

research.title: FreqDepthKV: Frequency-Guided Depth Sharing for Robust KV Cache Compression in Long-Context LLM Inference
research.summary: Anna Córdoba, Adam Puente Tercero, Nerea Angulo Hijo et al. — Long-context LLM inference is increasingly limited by the memory and bandwidth cost of KV caches, yet aggressive compression can remove the layer-specific evidence needed for retrieval and multi-step reasoning.

labs.title: The power of collaboration: How we can reduce traffic congestion
labs.summary: During “treatment” days, the modified routing guided all trips that encountered the pre-selected congested segments toward alternative routes with similar travel times. Estimated outcomes on trip travel times, speeds, and estimated emissions. Bottom: Histogram of net volume changes on individual segments.

labs.title: Australian Payments Plus moves faster with ChatGPT and Codex
labs.summary: See how Australian Payments Plus uses ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to move faster through payments complexity. AP+ saves time, improves quality, and keeps human judgment central.

labs.title: MUFG aims to become AI-native with OpenAI
labs.summary: MUFG uses ChatGPT Enterprise to build an AI-native organization, improve workflows, and deliver new AI-powered financial services at scale.

labs.title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
labs.summary: Today, Google DeepMind and A24 are announcing a first-of-its-kind partnership focused on research. This partnership creates a deep research and development collaboration between A24 and Google DeepMind spanning multiple projects over time. This hands-on collaboration provides Google DeepMind with invaluable feedback and guidance from leading artists.

labs.title: Expanding our Heat Resilience data to 50+ global cities
labs.summary: This dataset is being made accessible through a new high-resolution Heat Resilience Earth Engine App. This dataset is open and accessible through our new, high-resolution Heat Resilience Earth Engine App . This platform provides high-resolution rooftop albedo (reflectivity) data to empower proactive municipal planning.

labs.title: Start building with Nano Banana 2 Lite and Gemini Omni Flash
labs.summary: We’re making it easier to experiment and scale your ideas with Nano Banana 2 Lite, our fastest, most cost-efficient Gemini Image model, and Gemini Omni Flash for high-quality video generation and conversational editing. Today, Gemini Omni Flash (gemini-omni-flash-preview) is rolling to developers via the Gemini API and Google AI Studio, natively supporting high-quality video generation and conversational editing…

labs.title: Introducing TabFM: A zero-shot foundation model for tabular data
labs.summary: We introduce TabFM, a new foundation model for tabular data to simplify classification and regression workflows. Today, we introduce TabFM, a foundation model designed specifically for tabular data classification and regression. By framing tabular prediction as an ICL problem, TabFM eliminates the need for manual model training, hyperparameter tuning , and complex feature engineering.

labs.title: How ChatGPT adoption has expanded
labs.summary: New OpenAI Signals data shows how ChatGPT adoption is growing globally, with users increasing usage, exploring more capabilities, and driving growth across regions and languages.

labs.title: Core dump epidemiology: fixing an 18-year-old bug
labs.summary: OpenAI engineers used large-scale core dump analysis to debug rare infrastructure crashes, uncovering both a hardware fault and a long-standing software bug.

labs.title: Introducing GeneBench-Pro
labs.summary: Introducing GeneBench-Pro, a new benchmark testing AI performance in genomics, biology, and scientific research using complex, real-world datasets.

labs.title: Mapping Europe’s AI Workforce Opportunity
labs.summary: A new OpenAI report maps how AI could reshape jobs across the EU, highlighting which occupations may face automation, growth, or workflow changes.

startups.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
startups.summary: With ZML/LLMD , the newly launched LLM inference server, the company’s ambition is to break existing silos and make different chips available for AI use cases at their maximum available speed, and sometimes faster, ZML founder Steeve Morin told TechCrunch. Unlike ZML’s first public project, the inference-focused ML framework released in 2024 and updated in March , ZML/LLMD is not open source. “I couldn’t do ZML…

startups.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
startups.summary: “Having JPMorgan Chase decide they’re going to use SambaNova for their inference solution is a big deal,” Liang told TechCrunch. Liang said SambaNova’s edge as “premium inference” running the largest models and running them fast. SambaNova sees three types of customers.

startups.title: Final extension: Startup Battlefield Australia applications now close July 20
startups.summary: Due to overwhelming interest, we’ve extended applications for Startup Battlefield Australia to July 20 . Automatic entry into Startup Battlefield 200 at TechCrunch Disrupt in San Francisco this October. Isabelle leads Startup Battlefield, TechCrunch’s iconic launchpad and competition for the world’s most promising early-stage startups.

startups.title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
startups.summary: Meta on Tuesday unveiled Muse Image, its new AI image generator built by Meta Superintelligence Labs, the company’s dedicated AI unit. The model also features prompt-based image editing, which lets users create images to share across Meta’s apps and platforms. Meta’s privacy record is one reason for users’ unease over Muse.

startups.title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
startups.summary: It’s a new way to think about the relationship between frontier and open source models. In Zhang’s telling, they aren’t competitors, and open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. As more mature use cases switch to lighter models , new use cases keep arising — and the overall spend on frontier models barely goes down.

startups.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
startups.summary: The most recent example is Microsoft, which has reportedly begun to deploy a cost-savings strategy by relying less on software from OpenAI and Anthropic and instead deploying its own in-house models. Last month, at its annual Build conference, the company announced the launch of seven new MAI models , including an agentic coder and a text-to-image generator. Microsoft’s apparent cutbacks are part of a broader trend.

startups.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
startups.summary: Our systems flag content by matching it against known harmful material. The intended behavior is to… — Discord Support (@discord_support) July 7, 2026 Our systems flag content by matching it against known harmful material. I have requested a review of my suspension. @discord @discord_support pic.twitter.com/QfAkCIJo6S Discord isn’t alone in facing moderation troubles due to automated systems.

startups.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
startups.summary: Toward that end, it has acquired the team behind the vibe-coding and AI agent platform Bud (formerly Orchids). The Y Combinator-backed startup began as a vibe-coding platform letting users spin up apps for mobile, web, Slack, browser, and more. Last year, it released Figma Make for creating web apps .

startups.title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
startups.summary: After expanding its service to include live content, video games, and, more recently, video podcasts, the streamer is now adding video content from publishers such as BuzzFeed Studios, Condé Nast, Hearst Magazines, People Inc., Tastemade, and various Penske Media PMX brands, like Variety, THR, Billboard, Eater, Rolling Stone, and IndieWire. Netflix says other publishers will be added over time. To court viewers…

startups.title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: The worst breaches of 2026 so far
startups.summary: In the data breach, Klue exposed the keys to its customers’ cloud services, allowing the hackers to break in and steal those stores of data to extort those companies for a ransom. The hackers breached the company’s flagship learning management system Canvas to steal private data and personal information belonging to over 30 million students and staff. Instructure wasn’t the only company targeted by the ShinyHunters…

startups.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
startups.summary: Claude Cowork — Anthropic’s Claude Code-style agent for general knowledge work — is coming to your phone. Claude Cowork launched as a desktop app in January, but starting Tuesday it is available on web and mobile for Max subscribers. Anthropic recently launched Claude Tag , an always-on Claude that lives in Slack and acts as an AI teammate.

wider_tech.title: Of course viewers are giving up on Netflix shows
wider_tech.summary: Even though Netflix is the world’s most popular paid streaming service, the company has been struggling to keep viewers watching its series after their first seasons. In addition to viewers spending more time on other platforms, Netflix also has to contend with the way the streaming wars have fundamentally altered people’s relationships with episodic television. Something else that Netflix has to take into…

wider_tech.summary: Davit talks directly to Apple's open-source container daemon over XPC — the same wire path the CLI uses. No container platform installed? Set a port mapping — host 8088 → container 80 — and run. (307 points, 69 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: X says top accounts steal videos from other users as it announces new video tools
wider_tech.summary: The platform’s new built-in video editor and recorder are available now in X’s iOS app. The platform’s new built-in video editor and recorder are available now in X’s iOS app. The new video tools are X’s latest push to encourage more users to post original content.
